sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects the creation of potentially malicious script and executable files in Windows startup folders, which is a common persistence technique used by threat actors.
These files (.ps1, .vbs, .js, .bat, etc.) are automatically executed when a user logs in, making the Startup folder an attractive target for attackers.
This technique is frequently observed in malvertising campaigns and malware distribution where attackers attempt to maintain long-term access to compromised systems.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Disable Windows Defender Functionalities Via Registry Keys
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects when attackers or tools disable Windows Defender functionalities via the Windows registry
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: AlertIQ, Ján Trenčanský, frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Dot net compiler compiles file from suspicious location
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Dot net compiler compiles file from suspicious location

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Schtasks Creation Or Modification With SYSTEM Privileges
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation or update of a scheduled task to run with "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM" privileges

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Schedule Task Creation From Env Variable Or Potentially Suspicious Path Via Schtasks.EXE
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects Schtask creations that point to a suspicious folder or an environment variable often used by malware
Detects modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Victor Sergeev, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Gleb Sukhodolskiy, Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community, Tim Shelton, frack113 (split)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Disable Microsoft Defender Firewall via Registry
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Adversaries may disable or modify system firewalls in order to bypass controls limiting network usage
A General detection for files being created in the Windows startup directory. This could be an indicator of persistence.
Detects netsh commands that turns off the Windows firewall

values.ParentCommandLine: "C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe" /C netsh advfirewall set currentprofile state off
values.CommandLine: netsh  advfirewall set currentprofile state off

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects execution of netsh with the "advfirewall" and the "set" option in order to set new values for properties of a existing rule
Detects modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Victor Sergeev, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Gleb Sukhodolskiy, Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community, Tim Shelton, frack113 (split)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects execution of "csc.exe" to compile .NET code. Attackers often leverage this to compile code on the fly and use it in other stages.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Files With System Process Name In Unsuspected Locations
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects the creation of an executable with a system process name in folders other than the system ones (System32, SysWOW64, etc.).
It is highly recommended to perform an initial baseline before using this rule in production.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Sander Wiebing, Tim Shelton, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Schtasks Schedule Type With High Privileges
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects scheduled task creations or modification to be run with high privileges on a suspicious schedule type
Detects the creation of scheduled tasks by user accounts via the "schtasks" utility.

When C# is compiled dynamically, a .cmdline file will be created as a part of the process.
Certain processes are not typically observed compiling C# code, but can do so without touching disk.
This can be used to unpack a payload for execution

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_category: Domain Observed Used for C2 Detected
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: ET MALWARE Loki Locker Ransomware CnC Domain in DNS Lookup
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert dns $HOME_NET any -> any any (msg:"ET MALWARE Loki Locker Ransomware CnC Domain in DNS Lookup"; dns.query; content:"loki-locker.one"; nocase; bsize:15; reference:url,blogs.blackberry.com/en/2022/03/lokilocker-ransomware; reference:md5,8aea251877cb4f5ee6cf357831f8620c; reference:url,twitter.com/James_inthe_box/status/1504194638885711872; classtype:domain-c2; sid:2035511; rev:1; metadata:affected_product Windows_XP_Vista_7_8_10_Server_32_64_Bit, attack_target Client_Endpoint, created_at 2022_03_17, deployment Perimeter, performance_impact Low, confidence High, signature_severity Major, tag Ransomware, tag Description_Generated_By_Proofpoint_Nexus, updated_at 2022_03_17;)
